0b026a627194a453e904a46ff34d9ee8b71e31e2.reason: The flagged line generates a DES key via KeyGenerator.getInstance("DES") and uses it to initialize a Cipher whose algorithm comes from benchmark.properties, which is configured as DES/ECB/PKCS5Padding. In doPost, an attacker-controlled cookie value (request.getCookies -> URLDecoder.decode) flows through doSomething into 'bar', is encrypted with this DES/ECB cipher (c.doFinal), and the result is persisted to passwordFile.txt. DES (and ECB mode) provide inadequate encryption strength for sensitive data; there is no sanitization or mitigation for the weak algorithm, so the defect is real and reachable via a deployed servlet endpoint.

19488dd5a3fcd1c81de99e312819262d164583ac.reason: Although 'param' is attacker-controlled (request.getParameterMap at line 43), the helper doSomething never returns it: the condition (7 * 42) - 86 > 200 evaluates to 208 > 200, a compile-time constant true, so 'bar' is always the literal "This_should_always_happen" (lines 85-87). Therefore fileName = Utils.TESTFILES_DIR + a fixed constant (line 56), and the FileOutputStream sink at line 58 receives no attacker-controlled data. No path traversal is possible.

The taint from the request parameter is broken in doSomething(): (7*42)-86 evaluates to 208, which is always > 200, so bar is unconditionally assigned the constant string 'This_should_always_happen' and never the attacker-controlled param. Therefore fileName = TESTFILES_DIR + constant is not attacker-controlled at the FileOutputStream sink; the scanner's propagator hops through 'bar' are incorrect. No path traversal is possible.

4222e7168ebfdde4bd4cfeeaac2a3733b926f19a.reason: The servlet mapped to /ldapi-00/BenchmarkTest01568 reads attacker-controlled input via request.getParameterValues("BenchmarkTest01568") (line 43), assigns it to param (45), and passes it through Test.doSomething (48). doSomething (lines 112-116) only performs a base64 encode-then-decode round trip, returning the input unchanged — it strips nothing. The result is concatenated unsanitized into an LDAP search filter at line 57 ("(&(objectclass=person)(uid=" + bar + "))") and executed by ctx.search(base, filter, sc) at line 60. No encodeForLDAP or any other LDAP escaping occurs on this path; the ESAPI encoding in LDAPManager.java:108-111 belongs to a private, unused search method and does not apply. The repo's own expected-results file (expectedresults-1.2.csv:1569) also labels this test case a true LDAP-injection positive.

fe607636a96be919548da9ff0319c54680e3b220.reason: The servlet explicitly instantiates the SHA1 MessageDigest algorithm (line 56, with fallback at line 58) and uses it to hash data (md.update/md.digest at lines 74-76), writing the resulting hash to a file named 'passwordFile.txt' (lines 77-86). The flagged defect — use of the collision-broken SHA1 algorithm — is real and reachable whenever doGet/doPost executes; no configuration or conditional replaces SHA1 with a stronger algorithm. The exact input hashed (constant 'bob' via the switch on 'B' at lines 120-128) does not change the fact that a weak hash algorithm is deliberately selected and used.

The flagged defect is real and reachable: doPost unconditionally instantiates a SHA1 MessageDigest (lines 56 and 58), hashes a value treated as sensitive (md.update at line 74, md.digest at line 76), and stores the resulting hash in 'passwordFile.txt' (lines 77-86), i.e., a password-storage context where SHA1's lack of collision resistance matters. The algorithm string is hardcoded, so no configuration prevents the weak hash; the weakness is in the algorithm choice itself, which is exactly what CWE-328 flags. (In this particular flow the switch at lines 123-137 selects case 'B', so the hashed value is the constant 'bob', but the flagged defect — use of SHA1 for hashing sensitive values — is present and executed regardless.)
